metadata.name: Living Metal — Steel Guardian is a creature during p1's turn and can attack
metadata.description: Tests the Living Metal keyword ability (CR 702.161a) using Steel Guardian, a synthetic 3/3 Artifact Vehicle with Living Metal. During p1's turn, Living Metal applies at Layer 4 (TypeChange) and adds CardType::Creature to Steel Guardian while p1 is the active player. This is demonstrated behaviorally: p1 declares Steel Guardian as an attacker in the DeclareAttackers step — if the engine accepts the command, Steel Guardian is a creature. p2 has no blockers. Steel Guardian deals 3 combat damage to p2 (40 → 37). Proves that Living Metal grants creature status without the Vehicle being crewed.
metadata.generation_notes: CR 702.161a: 'Living Metal — During your turn, this permanent is an artifact creature in addition to its other types.' This is a static ability (CR 604.1) that generates a continuous effect applied at Layer 4 (TypeChange, CR 613.1d). The engine applies it in rules/layers.rs: when layer == EffectLayer::TypeChange AND the object has KeywordAbility::LivingMetal AND obj.zone == ZoneId::Battlefield AND state.turn.active_player == obj.controller, it inserts CardType::Creature into chars.card_types. Steel Guardian (cid: steel-guardian) has CardType::Artifact as its base type, P/T 3/3, and KeywordAbility::LivingMetal per its CardDefinition in crates/engine/src/cards/defs/steel_guardian.rs. The card also has Crew(2) but crewing is not used here — Living Metal provides creature status directly during p1's turn, allowing Steel Guardian to attack without any crew activation. The harness does NOT support a 'permanent.<name>.card_types' assertion path. The check_assertions function in crates/engine/tests/script_replay.rs only handles: players.*.life, players.*.poison_counters, zones.hand.*.count, zones.stack.count, zones.stack (is_empty), zones.graveyard.* (includes/excludes), zones.battlefield.* (includes/excludes), permanent.*.tapped, and permanent.*.counters.*. Unknown paths fall into the '_' arm and silently return None (no mismatch — they pass vacuously without checking). To avoid false assurance from vacuous passes, this script does NOT use card_type assertions. Instead it proves creature status behaviorally: if declare_attackers succeeds with Steel Guardian as the attacker, the engine accepted it as a creature. The 'not a creature during p2's turn' invariant is validated by the unit test test_living_metal_not_creature_during_opponent_turn in crates/engine/tests/living_metal.rs — that test directly calls calculate_characteristics() with active_player set to p2 and asserts CardType::Creature is absent. The initial state uses phase: 'declare_attackers' (p1 turn 4) — starting directly in the DeclareAttackers step avoids having to advance through multiple phases. Turn 4 ensures no summoning sickness (GameStateBuilder sets has_summoning_sickness: false for initial-state permanents). p2 controls no creatures, so Steel Guardian is unblocked and deals 3 combat damage.

script.step_note: p1's turn, DeclareAttackers step. Steel Guardian (Artifact Vehicle with Living Metal) is on p1's battlefield. Living Metal (CR 702.161a) applies at Layer 4: since p1 is the active player and p1 controls Steel Guardian, CardType::Creature is added. p1 declares Steel Guardian as an attacker — the engine's declare_attackers handler requires the permanent to have CardType::Creature, so accepting this command proves Living Metal is working.

actions.description: Initial: Steel Guardian untapped on p1's battlefield. Stack empty. Life totals 40/40.
actions.note: Steel Guardian was placed directly in the initial state (no summoning sickness at turn 4). Living Metal is a static ability — no activation required; it applies continuously while p1 is the active player.

CR 508.1: P1 declares Steel Guardian as an attacking creature targeting p2. CR 702.161a: Steel Guardian is an artifact creature during p1's turn due to Living Metal — the engine accepts this only because CardType::Creature was added by the Layer 4 static effect. CR 508.1a: Steel Guardian is untapped and free of summoning sickness (placed in initial state at turn 4; builder sets has_summoning_sickness: false). CR 508.1f: Steel Guardian becomes tapped as a side effect of attacking.

actions.description: After declare_attackers: Steel Guardian is tapped (attacking). Stack empty. The command acceptance is behavioral proof that Living Metal granted creature status during p1's turn.
actions.note: CR 508.1f: Steel Guardian is tapped as part of attacking. The engine's declare_attackers handler rejects non-creature permanents — acceptance here confirms CardType::Creature was present via Living Metal.
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority with empty stack → advance to DeclareBlockers step.

script.step_note: P2 declares no blockers (controls no creatures). Steel Guardian becomes an unblocked attacker.

actions.note: CR 509.1: P2 declares no blockers (controls no creatures). CR 509.1h: Steel Guardian has no blockers declared → it becomes an unblocked attacker.
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority → advance to CombatDamage step.

script.step_note: CombatDamage step: Steel Guardian (power 3, unblocked) assigns 3 combat damage to p2 (CR 510.1b). P2 life: 40 → 37. No SBAs from combat.

actions.note: CR 704.5a: p2 has 37 life — no player-death SBA. CR 704.5g: Steel Guardian took 0 damage — no lethal-damage SBA. No SBAs apply.
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority after CombatDamage. Stack empty → advance to EndOfCombat.

actions.description: Final state: p2 at 37 life (took 3 combat damage from unblocked Steel Guardian). Steel Guardian still on p1's battlefield (tapped from attacking). Stack empty.
actions.note: CR 510.1b: Unblocked Steel Guardian (power 3) dealt 3 combat damage to p2. P2 life: 40 → 37. Steel Guardian remains on the battlefield tapped. The 'not a creature during p2's turn' invariant is validated by unit tests in crates/engine/tests/living_metal.rs.
